First woman to fly solo across the Atlantic?
What was the Cold War?
200.a: Geopolitical tension between the USA and Soviet Union from 1947 to 1991

200.q: Who built the Colosseum?
200.a: The Roman Empire — under Emperor Vespasian, completed 80 AD

200.q: Year the Berlin Wall fell?
Who was Napoleon Bonaparte?
200.a: French military leader and emperor who dominated Europe in the early 19th century

200.q: What is the Renaissance?
200.a: European cultural movement from 14th to 17th century meaning 'rebirth'

200.q: Who was Julius Caesar?
200.a: Roman general and statesman assassinated on the Ides of March, 44 BC

400.q: Which civilization built Chichen Itza?
400.a: The Maya

400.q: Which city was hit by the first atomic bomb in war?
400.a: Hiroshima

400.q: Who was the first man in space?
400.a: Yuri Gagarin

400.q: What empire was ruled by Suleiman the Magnificent?
400.a: Ottoman Empire

400.q: Who was the first Tudor king of England?
400.a: Henry VII

400.q: Which war was fought between the North and South in the United States?
400.a: American Civil War

400.q: Who was the longest-reigning British monarch?
400.a: Elizabeth II

400.q: What treaty ended World War I?
400.a: Treaty of Versailles

400.q: Who was assassinated in Sarajevo in 1914, sparking WWI?
400.a: Archduke Franz Ferdinand

400.q: What empire was Genghis Khan the founder of?
400.a: Mongol Empire

400.q: What event began on D-Day, June 6, 1944?
400.a: Allied invasion of Normandy

400.q: What was the Manhattan Project?
400.a: Development of the atomic bomb

400.q: Who led the Soviet Union during the Cuban Missile Crisis?
400.a: Nikita Khrushchev

400.q: What was the Renaissance?
400.a: A cultural rebirth in Europe from the 14th-17th century

400.q: What was the Spanish Armada?
400.a: A fleet sent by Spain to invade England in 1588

400.q: What city was the capital of the Byzantine Empire?
400.a: Constantinople

400.q: Who was the first female Prime Minister of the UK?
400.a: Margaret Thatcher

400.q: What revolution began in Russia in 1917?
400.a: Bolshevik Revolution

400.q: Which country was first to give women the vote?
400.a: New Zealand (1893)

400.q: Last Pharaoh of ancient Egypt?
400.a: Cleopatra VII

400.q: Year India gained independence?
Which civilization built Machu Picchu?
400.a: The Inca Empire

400.q: Who painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling?
400.a: Michelangelo

400.q: What was the Manhattan Project?
400.a: US-led WWII research project that produced the first nuclear weapons

400.q: What was the Silk Road?
400.a: An ancient network of trade routes connecting China to the Mediterranean

400.q: What was the Black Death?
400.a: Devastating plague pandemic (1347–1351) killing ~1/3 of Europe's population

400.q: What was the Boston Tea Party?
400.a: 1773 political protest — American colonists threw British tea into Boston Harbor

400.q: Who was Nelson Mandela?
400.a: South Africa's first Black president — spent 27 years in prison fighting apartheid

400.q: What was the Battle of Thermopylae?
400.a: 480 BC — 300 Spartans held off a massive Persian army for three days

400.q: What was Operation Overlord?
400.a: The codename for the D-Day invasion of Normandy, June 6, 1944

400.q: Who built the Taj Mahal?
400.a: Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan — built in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal

400.q: What was the Scramble for Africa?
400.a: Rapid colonisation of Africa by European powers between 1881 and 1914

400.q: Who wrote The Communist Manifesto?
400.a: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels (1848)

400.q: What was the Meiji Restoration?
400.a: Japan's rapid industrialisation and modernisation under Emperor Meiji from 1868

400.q: What was the significance of the Battle of Waterloo?
400.a: Napoleon's final defeat in 1815 — ending the Napoleonic Wars and his rule

400.q: Who was Simón Bolívar?
400.a: South American leader who led independence movements against Spain in the early 1800s

400.q: What was the Cultural Revolution in China?
400.a: Mao Zedong's 1966–1976 campaign to purge capitalist and traditional elements from Chinese society

400.q: Who was Alexander the Great?
400.a: Macedonian king who conquered one of the largest empires in history by age 30

400.q: What was the Transatlantic Slave Trade?
400.a: Transportation of enslaved Africans to the Americas between the 16th and 19th centuries

400.q: What was the Treaty of Versailles?
400.a: 1919 peace treaty that ended World War I and imposed harsh penalties on Germany

400.q: Who was Joan of Arc?
400.a: French heroine who led forces to victory at Orléans (1429) — burned at the stake

400.q: What was the Rwandan Genocide?
400.a: 1994 mass slaughter of ~800,000 Tutsi people by Hutu-led government in 100 days

400.q: What was the significance of the Battle of Hastings?
400.a: 1066 — William the Conqueror defeated King Harold II, beginning Norman rule of England

400.q: Who was Genghis Khan?
400.a: Founder of the Mongol Empire — the largest contiguous land empire in history

400.q: What was the Haitian Revolution?
400.a: 1791–1804 slave rebellion making Haiti the first Black republic and first nation to permanently abolish slavery

400.q: What was the significance of the Gutenberg Bible?
400.a: First major book printed with movable type (c.1455) — revolutionised the spread of information in Europe

400.q: Who was Suleiman the Magnificent?
400.a: Tenth Ottoman Sultan (1520–1566) who presided over the empire's golden age

400.q: What was the Partition of India?
400.a: 1947 division of British India into two independent nations — India and Pakistan

600.q: What ship carried the Pilgrims to North America in 1620?
600.a: The Mayflower

600.q: Who was the Carthaginian general who crossed the Alps with elephants?
600.a: Hannibal Barca

600.q: What Chinese dynasty built much of the Forbidden City?
600.a: The Ming dynasty

600.q: Which 1783 treaty ended the American Revolutionary War?
600.a: The Treaty of Paris

600.q: Which English king was executed in 1649?
600.a: Charles I

600.q: What Newton book laid the foundation of classical mechanics in 1687?
600.a: Principia Mathematica

600.q: What treaty ended the Thirty Years' War in 1648?
600.a: Peace of Westphalia

600.q: Who led the Soviet Union after Lenin?
600.a: Joseph Stalin

600.q: What civilization used cuneiform writing?
600.a: Sumerians

600.q: Which city fell to the Ottomans in 1453?
600.a: Constantinople

600.q: Who was the British prime minister for most of World War II?
600.a: Winston Churchill

600.q: What year did the Battle of Hastings happen?
Which Chinese dynasty built much of the present Great Wall?
600.a: Ming dynasty

600.q: Who was the queen of Egypt allied with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony?
600.a: Cleopatra

600.q: What rebellion was led by Spartacus?
600.a: Third Servile War

600.q: Who was the first emperor of Rome?
600.a: Augustus

600.q: Which empire controlled present-day Peru before the Spanish conquest?
600.a: Inca Empire

600.q: Who wrote the Ninety-five Theses?
600.a: Martin Luther

600.q: Which battle in 1815 ended Napoleon's rule for good?
600.a: Waterloo

600.q: Who was the last tsar of Russia?
600.a: Nicholas II

600.q: Which dynasty ruled China during Zheng He's voyages?
600.a: Ming dynasty

600.q: Who was the Sun King of France?
600.a: Louis XIV

600.q: What was the Taiping Rebellion?
600.a: A massive civil war in China (1850-1864)

600.q: What was the Congress of Vienna?
600.a: 1814-1815 conference to reorganize Europe after Napoleon

600.q: Who was Saladin?
600.a: Muslim leader who recaptured Jerusalem from the Crusaders

600.q: What was the Meiji Restoration?
600.a: 1868 political revolution modernizing Japan

600.q: What was the Scramble for Africa?
600.a: European colonization of Africa in the late 1800s

600.q: What was the Dreyfus Affair?
600.a: A French political scandal involving anti-Semitism (1894)

600.q: Who was Hatshepsut?
600.a: One of the few female pharaohs of Egypt

600.q: What was the War of the Roses?
600.a: English civil wars between House of Lancaster and York (1455-1487)

600.q: What year did Constantinople fall to the Ottomans?
Who led the Haitian Revolution?
600.a: Toussaint Louverture

600.q: What was the Boxer Rebellion?
600.a: Anti-foreign uprising in China (1899-1901)

600.q: Who founded the Ottoman Empire?
600.a: Osman I

600.q: What was the significance of the Battle of Tours (732)?
600.a: Charles Martel halted Muslim expansion into Europe

600.q: Who was the Byzantine Emperor who codified Roman law?
600.a: Emperor Justinian I (Corpus Juris Civilis)

600.q: What was the Shogun in Japanese history?
600.a: Military dictator who wielded actual power while the Emperor was a ceremonial figurehead

600.q: Who was Genghis Khan's most famous grandson?
600.a: Kublai Khan — who founded the Yuan dynasty in China

600.q: What was the Long March?
600.a: Mao Zedong's 1934–1935 strategic retreat of Chinese Communist forces — ~6,000 miles

600.q: Who was Nicolaus Copernicus?
600.a: Renaissance astronomer who first proposed the Sun, not Earth, is the centre of the solar system

600.q: What was the Thirty Years War?
600.a: A devastating Central European war (1618–1648) starting as religious conflict then political

600.q: What was the Sykes-Picot Agreement?
600.a: 1916 secret agreement between Britain and France dividing the Middle East — its borders still cause conflict

600.q: What was the significance of the Rosetta Stone?
600.a: A 196 BC decree in three scripts — it allowed scholars to decode Egyptian hieroglyphics

600.q: What was the Spanish Inquisition?
600.a: Tribunal established in 1478 to maintain Catholic orthodoxy — known for torture and persecution

600.q: What was the Opium War?
600.a: Two wars (1839–1842, 1856–1860) where Britain and France forced China to open trade and legalise opium

600.q: Who was Ivan the Terrible?
600.a: Russia's first Tsar (1547–1584) — modernised Russia but killed his own son in rage

600.q: What was the significance of the Council of Nicaea?
600.a: 325 AD — first ecumenical Christian council, established the Nicene Creed and standardised doctrine

600.q: What was the Taiping Rebellion?
600.a: Massive civil war in China (1850–1864) — 20–30 million deaths, one of history's deadliest conflicts

600.q: What was the significance of the Black Death's long-term impact?
600.a: Killed 30–60% of Europe — led to labour shortages, peasant uprisings, decline of feudalism, eventually the Renaissance

600.q: Who was Simon de Montfort?
600.a: Earl of Leicester who created England's first Parliament in 1265 — often called father of Parliament

600.q: What was the significance of the Library of Alexandria?
600.a: One of the largest libraries of the ancient world — containing scrolls from across the known world

600.q: What was the significance of the 1905 Russian Revolution?
600.a: A wave of mass unrest and strikes that forced Tsar Nicholas II to create a constitution and parliament

600.q: What was the Korean War?
600.a: A 1950–1953 conflict where North Korea (Chinese-backed) fought South Korea (UN/US-backed) — ended in armistice

600.q: What was Operation Barbarossa?
600.a: Nazi Germany's massive invasion of the Soviet Union in June 1941 — the largest military operation in history

600.q: What is the significance of 1453 in history?
600.a: The fall of Constantinople to the Ottoman Turks — ending the Byzantine Empire and the Middle Ages

600.q: Who was Cleopatra's relationship with Roman leaders?
600.a: She had relationships with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony — strategic alliances to protect Egypt

600.q: What was the significance of the Peace of Westphalia (1648)?
600.a: It ended the Thirty Years War and established the modern concept of state sovereignty
